title: limonium angustifolium
intro: Limonium angustifolium, also known as sea lavender or narrow-leaved sea lavender, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Plumbaginaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Limonium angustifolium include teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to soothe skin irritations and promote overall well-being.
